Parish Parish Of Of Drumragh Drumragh Parish Parish Of Of Drumragh Drumragh
Fifteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time 11th July 2021
The phrase ‘Every day is a school day’ is often used to reflect the fact that we are
constantly learning and evolving throughout our lives. Having been living through the
Covid 19 pandemic, most of us would probably now describe it as a ‘life changing
event’. Many of us are lucky to have come through it relatively unscathed, but we
mourn with those who have lost loved ones. There is also a lot of fear of what might
happen next, as regards this virus and its implications for our future. So, maybe it is
more important than ever to look at what we might have learned from the experience of
the last year. What is obvious is that we have developed a new-found respect for our
key workers – those working in the NHS, care workers, factory workers, shop workers,
delivery drivers, clergy, undertakers, etc. We are so thankful for all the people who kept
everything going while we stayed cocooned in our houses, doing our bit to halt the
spread of the virus. What is also evident is that people have stepped up to help their
communities by volunteering and helping to look after the elderly and vulnerable who
were unable to leave their homes.
Let us build on this sense of community going forward and make the sentiments felt
during this pandemic commonplace. Let us not wait for another outbreak of this awful
virus before we look in on our neighbours or volunteer our services. In a world where
we can be anything, let us be kind. Could our return to ‘normal’ mean a new normal of
kindness and respect for others that maybe was sadly lacking in the past? Could our
new ‘normal’ be a better way of life for all of us, with consideration for others coming
as second nature? If so, please let ‘normal service’ resume.

Certificate in Christian Thought - Two-year part-time course
The Drumalis Retreat Centre are offering a two year part-time intensive course in
Christian Thought, starting in September 2021. The Certificate in Christian Thought
programme aims to offer a space where adults can explore, reflect upon, discuss and
study faith and how it relates to life in today's world, both at a personal and
This course is offered to men and women from all walks of life. It sees participants'
lived experience as complementary to the more formal input of the sessions and
values the sharing of that experience as one of the great gifts participants can offer to
each other. It presents Christian spirituality and theology in an accessible, down-to-
earth way in an accepting and relaxed atmosphere.
It is possible to do the course for validation by St Mary's University College or for
The course will be delivered over 7 Saturdays in each of two successive years, from
10:00am until 4:30pm. This represents 70 hours of direct contact with the expectation
of a minimum of 40 hours of home study and guided reading.
